# GitHub Repo Audit — 2026-06-30

**Repo:** danieljustus/symaira-print  **Default branch:** main  **Visibility:** public

## Summary

| Category    | PASS | WARN | FAIL |
|-------------|------|------|------|
| Repo        |   8  |   1  |   0  |
| Community   |  20  |   2  |   0  |
| Protection  |   6  |   4  |   0  |
| Security    |   6  |   0  |   0  |
| CI          |   6  |   0  |   0  |
| Labels      |   8  |   0  |   0  |
| Release     |   0  |   1  |   0  |

No `FAIL` findings this run — the repo is in good baseline shape. All findings below are `WARN`.

## Findings

- [ ] **[Release/WARN] No release has been published yet**
  - **Status quo:** `gh release list` returns empty and no git tags exist, even though `README.md` already promises `go install ...@latest`, a Homebrew tap, and links a "Releases" badge to `releases/latest`. The tag-triggered `.github/workflows/release.yml` (added 2026-06-30) is in place and was locally verified via `goreleaser release --snapshot`, but has never actually run against a real tag.
  - **Proposed solution:** Cut the first release: `/06-gh-prerelease` (gate) → `/07-gh-release`, pushing a `v0.1.0`-style tag (matching the `v0.1.0` milestone already closed at 25/25 issues) so the release workflow runs end-to-end for the first time and the README's install instructions become true.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact.

- [ ] **[Protection/WARN] `required_linear_history` not enabled despite squash-merge convention**
  - **Status quo:** Branch protection on `main` does not set `required_linear_history`, while `CONTRIBUTING.md` documents squash-merge as the convention and `squashMergeAllowed`/`mergeCommitAllowed`/`rebaseMergeAllowed` are all still `true` at the repo level. A merge-commit merge would silently break the documented linear-history expectation.
  - **Proposed solution:** Enable `required_linear_history` on the `main` branch protection rule (`gh api -X PUT repos/danieljustus/symaira-print/branches/main/protection/required_linear_history`), or disable `mergeCommitAllowed`/`rebaseMergeAllowed` at the repo level to make squash the only option.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Trivial effort / low impact.

- [ ] **[Protection/WARN] `require_code_owner_reviews` disabled despite an active `CODEOWNERS` file**
  - **Status quo:** `.github/CODEOWNERS` exists and parses without errors, but `required_pull_request_reviews.require_code_owner_reviews` is `false` on the `main` branch protection rule, so the file currently has no enforcement effect.
  - **Proposed solution:** Enable `require_code_owner_reviews` on the branch protection rule. Note this repo is solo (0 other write-access collaborators), so combine carefully with the existing `required_approving_review_count: 0` setting to avoid a self-blocking review requirement.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Trivial effort / low impact.

- [ ] **[Protection/WARN] `dismiss_stale_reviews` disabled**
  - **Status quo:** `required_pull_request_reviews.dismiss_stale_reviews` is `false`, so an approval does not get invalidated by new pushes to the PR branch.
  - **Proposed solution:** Enable `dismiss_stale_reviews` on the `main` branch protection rule.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Trivial effort / low impact.

- [ ] **[Protection/WARN] `enforce_admins` disabled**
  - **Status quo:** `enforce_admins.enabled` is `false`, so the repo admin (the only collaborator) can bypass the required-checks/conversation-resolution gates that apply to everyone else.
  - **Proposed solution:** Enable `enforce_admins` for a strict gate, or leave as-is if intentional admin bypass is wanted for emergency fixes — this is a WARN, not a FAIL, specifically because the bypass can be a deliberate choice.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Trivial effort / low impact.

- [ ] **[Repo/WARN] `autoMergeAllowed` is disabled**
  - **Status quo:** `gh api repos/danieljustus/symaira-print --jq .allow_auto_merge` returns `false`. The PR workflow conditionally arms native auto-merge (`gh pr merge --auto`) when the only remaining blocker is a transient approval/check-pending state, but that path is unavailable while this setting is off.
  - **Proposed solution:** `gh repo edit --enable-auto-merge` to allow the existing conditional auto-merge logic to actually engage when needed.
  - **Effort/Impact:** Trivial effort / low impact.

- [ ] **[Community/WARN] README has no screenshot, GIF, or terminal recording**
  - **Status quo:** No `![...](...)`/`<img>` matches and no `asciinema`/`asciicast` reference in `README.md`. For a CLI tool whose entire value proposition is PDF output quality (DIN 5008 letters, PDF/A-2a, PDF/UA-1), the README currently has zero visual proof.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add a sample rendered PDF screenshot (e.g. the `behoerde` profile output) near the top, and/or an asciinema recording of `symprint render` under "Quick start".
  - **Effort/Impact:** Low effort / high impact (READMEs without imagery lose significant click-through).

## Conclusion

The repo has no blocking (`FAIL`) findings — community files are at 100% health, security features are fully enabled with zero open alerts, CI is green with pinned actions and least-privilege permissions, and labels/Projects v2 are fully wired up. The top three fixes by impact: (1) cut the first release now that `v0.1.0` is feature-complete and the release workflow is in place, (2) add a README screenshot/recording to make the PDF-quality value proposition visible, (3) tighten branch protection (`required_linear_history`, `require_code_owner_reviews`, `dismiss_stale_reviews`) to match the conventions already documented in `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `CODEOWNERS`.
